cycles.campaign: Wesley - Timeshare Exits - Facebook

challengers.description: Editorial VSL with all refi learnings: institutional authority framing, blue CTA (not green), cycle-based scarcity, 10min reservation timer, loss-aversion in body copy, concrete stat boxes, autoplay muted video

Quiz format cloned from refi RF3D-AA champion pattern: 3 non-overlapping questions (ownership duration, prior exit attempts, aspirational outcome), answer-echo headline on results, 10min reservation timer, consumer protection eligibility framing

cycles.notes: First timeshare lander test. No baseline champion — both are new. Testing editorial VSL vs quiz format. Quiz questions designed to NOT overlap with offer page (gettimesharefree.com) which already asks: resort name, misled?, mortgage payments?, maintenance fees, financial burden.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Nutra-style editorial: historian discovers ancient Korean manuscripts linking to modern peptide patches
Social proof + scarcity angle headline and CTA

cycles.failure_reason: 0 conversions across all 3 landers after 114 clicks ($168 spent). Root cause: CTA links went directly to Shopify instead of through RT offer URL — conversion tracking was broken. Champion also had 0 LP views (Framer page missing track.js). Challenger A violated 1-3 component change rule (changed 4).

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm
cycles.note: No champion — all 3 are challengers testing against each other after cycle-001 failure. Cycles 003-004 not recorded in this file (gap). Superseded by cycle-005 which promoted challenger-o.

challengers.description: Ingredient science angle — dermatologists say patches beat creams, with comparison table
30-day honest review angle — first-person timeline showing day-by-day results with verdict box
Price comparison angle — $0.38/treatment vs $3-5 eye creams with annual savings calculator

fixes_from_cycle_001: CTA links now route through landing.olenis.com/703/01 (RT offer URL) instead of directly to Shopify
All landers have RT track.js script for LP view tracking
All landers have clickid passthrough JS (captures sub2/clickid/rt_clickid from URL, appends to CTA)
Each challenger changes only 2 components (headline + body_sections) for cleaner attribution
Dropped Framer champion (0 LP views) — all landers now on Netlify

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Dual scarcity — 3min timer + stock counter (11→3)
Pain-first quiz questions + O's urgency results page

cycles.note: Q and R never got proper traffic — campaign ran O at 100% for most of the period. Force-ended due to 7+ days without significance. O declared probable winner.

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — force-ended (O had 23%+ CVR edge over Q, 175% over R)

learnings: Practical quiz questions (concern, spend, time) outperform pain-first questions (duration, failed products, frustration) — 2.86% vs 1.04% CVR
Single 5-min timer beats dual scarcity (timer + stock counter) — 2.86% vs 2.31% CVR
Pain-first quiz disconnects from the 95%+ match score — users don't understand why frustration level = product compatibility
Dual urgency signals (timer + stock) feel manipulative for a digital product

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: New video background — closeup results v2 (Veo) replacing original video
Science headline ('97% Peptide Compatibility Detected') + batch scarcity ('Ships Within 24 Hours') replacing countdown timer

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (O had infinite CVR edge over S at 0%, 443% over T at 0.46%)

learnings: Veo closeup results video killed conversion — 0 conv on 104 clicks vs O's 2.50% CVR with original video. Video quality/mood during quiz affects downstream trust even though hidden on results page.
Replacing countdown timer with static 'Ships Within 24 Hours' batch scarcity dropped CVR from 2.50% to 0.46%. Countdown timer is the core urgency driver.
Science headline '97% Peptide Compatibility Detected' underperformed value headline 'Qualifies You for 50% Off'. Users need to see WHAT THEY GET, not what was detected.
LP CTR strongly correlates with urgency: O (25.4%) >> S (18.0%) = T (17.5%). Timer drives clicks.
Original video (final_2_wrrjdp.mp4) is proven — do not swap without strong reason.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Readable analyzer text (75% white + 95% white done state vs 50% + navy) + transactional CTA ('Get My Patches — 50% Off Applied')
Competitive ranking qualify msg ('top 3% of quiz takers') + prominent guarantee badge with checkmark icon below CTA

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (O had infinite CVR edge over U and V at 0%, early termination day 1)

learnings: Transactional CTA 'Get My Patches — 50% Off Applied' got highest LP CTR (21.6%) but 0 conversions. Implies deal is done, then Shopify checkout creates expectation mismatch. Urgency-claim CTA wins.

'Top 3% of quiz takers' qualify message killed LP CTR to 7.7% (vs O at 16.2%). Unbelievable ranking from 3 simple questions triggers BS detectors. Threshold-based qualify ('Only scores above 90%') is more credible.
Prominent guarantee badge with bordered card draws attention AWAY from CTA. Plain text guarantee is subordinate and works better — reassures without competing for clicks.
O's conversion engine is built on compressed decision architecture: score → exclusive unlock → offer → ticking timer → one clear action. Both challengers disrupted this compression.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Featured product card (100px image, Best Seller tag, inline star rating) + coral CTA button (#D4836B) for visual contrast
Review snippet above CTA ('I saw results after just 3 uses' — Sarah M.) + recency hero count ('2,347 women took this quiz today')

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (W had highest EPC $0.73 vs X $0.49 vs O $0.21; W and X tied on CVR ~1.75%, both 100%+ over O at 0.79%)

learnings: Featured product card (100px centered image, Best Seller badge, star rating) outperformed compact inline row on EPC ($0.73 vs $0.21). Creates mini-PDP experience that primes user for purchase before clicking through to Shopify.
Coral CTA (#D4836B) provides visual contrast against navy/cream palette. Combined with featured card, LP CTR similar to O (20.5% vs 20%) but higher checkout completion.
Review snippet (X) drove highest LP CTR at 30.3% but lower EPC ($0.49) — social proof drives more clicks but less purchase intent than product presentation.
Recency hero count ('2,347 women took this quiz today') may contribute to X's performance but confounded with review snippet — testing isolated in cycle-009.
Champion O's 5-cycle reign ends. Its compressed decision architecture remains the template, but product presentation needed upgrading.
Product presentation quality correlates with checkout rate; social proof correlates with click-through rate. Combining both is the next test.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Personalized headline echoing Q1 answer ('Your Dark Circle Match: Qualifies for 50% Off') + review snippet before coral CTA (from X)
Recency hero count ('2,347 women took this quiz today') + clinical proof in offer card ('Visible results after 3 uses — Clinical Study')

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (Y had 36%+ CVR lift over W, 28%+ over Z)

learnings: Personalized headline echoing Q1 concern ('Your Dark Circle Match') creates commitment-consistency loop — user sees their own input reflected back, making the result feel tailored. 3.21% CVR vs W at 2.36%.
Review snippet before CTA (from X) combined with personalization outperforms either alone. Y's 3.21% CVR is the highest in 10 cycles.

Recency hero count ('2,347 women today') + clinical proof in offer card (Z) didn't outperform generic lifetime count + featured product card (W). Clinical proof replaces the mini-PDP product image which may reduce visual product confirmation.
Hero daily count tested twice now (X in cycle-008, Z in cycle-009) — 0 wins, 2 losses. Recency framing doesn't add conversion value over lifetime social proof ('43,893 women').
RT had Y at 100% weight for part of the period (config mismatch). Data is directional, not clean A/B. Y's 36%+ CVR lift exceeds the 20% tie-breaking threshold.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Personalized analyzer steps echoing Q1 concern during loading animation + personalized CTA ('Get My [Concern] Fix — 50% Off Ends Soon')
Concern-matched review snippet (dynamic review based on Q1 answer) + live purchase notification toasts (2 toasts at 5s and 13s after results load)

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (32% CVR lift over Y, low confidence, no statistical significance)

learnings: Full personalization thread (analyzer + headline + CTA all echoing Q1) creates strongest commitment-consistency loop. AA's personalized analyzer steps + personalized CTA outperformed Y's generic analyzer + generic CTA.
Personalized CTA self-selects for higher-quality clicks: AA had 16.0% LP CTR vs Y's 24.7%, but AA's checkout rate was 16.7% vs Y's 8.1%. Fewer but better clicks.
Concern-matched dynamic reviews (AB) likely trigger skepticism — review that perfectly matches your concern feels manufactured.
Toast purchase notifications (AB) are a negative signal for this audience/price point. Lowest LP CTR at 14.4%.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Q2-echo value contrast in offer card + urgency consequence text below CTA
Review snippet moved above offer card + minimal clean offer (no Best Seller tag, no stars)

cycles.failure_reason: Campaign rotation was cleared by user. AC/AD only got 105 clicks each (2.8% of traffic instead of 33%). 4 unknown lander IDs were added outside optimizer absorbing 20% of traffic. 35% went to no lander. Test data compromised.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Dollar-specific CTA ($185K Available) + 15min timer + security above CTA
3-step verification animation + dollar range $147K-$185K
Concrete financial quiz (5yr ownership, $400K balance, 4 uses) + cycle-based scarcity
Loss-aversion result (red 'Missing Out') + 3-column benefit row above quiz

variants_tested: RF3D-Y: 8.02% CVR, $1.92 EPC (786 clicks, 63 conv) — WINNER
RF3D-Z: 6.58% CVR, $1.58 EPC (228 clicks, 15 conv) — WORST

learnings: Concrete financial quiz questions (5yr ownership, balance <$400K, intended use with 4 choices) create personalization bridge — user expects tailored result.
Cycle-based scarcity ('reviewed this cycle') is more credible than deadline scarcity in gov-style framework.
Dollar-specific CTA '$185K Available' creates expectation mismatch at lead form — user expects check, gets form.
3-step verification adds 3.6s processing time vs spinner — causes drop-off without added value.
Low anchor in range ($147K-$185K) reduces perceived value vs flat 'Up to $185K'.
Loss-aversion frame (red amount + 'Stop Losing Money') creates cognitive dissonance after passing eligibility quiz.
Benefit row above quiz adds visual complexity without decision-relevant info at that stage.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Result headline echoes Q1 answer ('Based on 5+ Years of Homeownership') + 10min reservation timer
Applicant counter ('2,847 homeowners checked this week') + urgency CTA ('Claim My Cash-Out Estimate — Slots Filling Fast')

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (AA had 39.7% relative CVR lift over Y, p=0.098)
variants_tested: RF3D-Y: 7.12% CVR, $1.71 EPC (646 clicks, 46 conv) — champion dethroned
RF3D-AA: 9.95% CVR, $2.28 EPC (432 clicks, 43 conv) — WINNER
RF3D-AB: 6.87% CVR, $1.47 EPC (131 clicks, 9 conv) — NOT in RT rotation, losing money

learnings: Answer-echo headline ('Based on 5+ Years of Homeownership') creates personalization continuity — user feels result is tailored to THEIR situation. 9.95% vs 7.12% CVR.
10-min reservation timer is congruent with gov screening framework — 'eligibility result expires' is believable. Creates urgency without contradicting positive result.
Applicant counter ('2,847 homeowners') positioned ABOVE result badge interrupts eligibility → amount → action flow. Users want THEIR answer, not social proof at that moment.
'Claim My Cash-Out Estimate — Slots Filling Fast' CTA breaks institutional tone. 'View My Personalized Offer →' maintains official screening voice. LP CTR dropped from 30% to 22%.
RF3D-AB was never properly added to RT rotation (only Y + AA at 50/50) — config/RT mismatch. Always verify RT campaign after cycle setup.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Q3-echo result description (dynamic benefit framing based on Q3 answer) + green CTA button (#2e8540) matching eligible badge
Highlighted amount card (green bg + border + 'Estimated Available Amount' label) + 5-min reservation timer

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — probable winner (AA had 41.9% relative CVR lift over AC, p=0.14)
variants_tested: RF3D-AA: 10.13% CVR, $2.43 EPC (375 clicks, 38 conv) — CHAMPION HOLDS
RF3D-AC: 7.14% CVR, $1.71 EPC (378 clicks, 27 conv) — Q3 echo desc + green CTA lost
RF3D-AD: 8.05% CVR, $1.93 EPC (87 clicks, 7 conv) — NOT in rotation (RT bug)

learnings: Green CTA (#2e8540) creates semantic confusion in gov framework — green is STATUS (eligible badge), not ACTION. Higher LP CTR (33%) but lower CVR (7.14% vs 10.13%). Users read green as 'already approved'.
Q3-echo result description re-engages rational brain at worst moment. Adding specific financial copy between eligibility high and CTA creates cognitive speed bump.
Blue CTA (#0071bc) matches institutional language throughout the page (header, progress bar, hover states). Feels like natural next step in screening flow.
Generic result description keeps focus on ELIGIBILITY STATUS, funneling directly to action. Minimal text between emotional high and CTA = higher conversion.
AD was never added to RT rotation (3rd lander dropped, same as cycle-002). Process fix: mandatory rt_get_campaign verification after every rt_update_campaign call.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Compressed flow — removed description paragraph, moved timer above CTA
Q3 motivation-echo headline (dynamic based on intended use) + deadline clock timer (real time instead of countdown)

results.notes: AE worst ever (5.45% CVR, removed description). AF Q3-echo significantly worse than Q1-echo (6.96%, p<0.05). AA confirmed.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Conversational Q2 + multi-step processing animation
Speed subtitle + emotional Q3 options

results.notes: Both AG (p=0.53) and AH (p=0.76) statistical ties. Mid-quiz changes have ZERO measurable effect. AA re-confirmed as champion.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Question headline + social proof
State-based Q1 + 3-min timer

results.notes: AI statistical tie (7.06%, p=0.48). AJ probable loser (4.88%, 39% CVR drop, underfunded 164 clicks). Neither entry-point change improved performance.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Card title as question with dollar anchor + positive alert bar (increased limits framing)
Step-progress card title + range anchor subtitle ($75K-$185K, 'in your area')

early_cuts.reason: Worst on every metric after 313 clicks: 7.03% CVR, $1.69 EPC, $101 profit. Step-progress title + range anchor underperformed.

results.significance: no — statistical tie (X²=0.113, p=0.74). AK trended better on all metrics but within 10%. Champion retained.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Peptide Eye Patches - Facebook - Ecomm

challengers.description: Concern-specific Q3 follow-up (puffiness/dark circles/fine lines/crepey/all get different Q3) + personalized offer subtitle (Targets [concern] in as few as 3 uses)
Outcome-focused hero headline (What If You Could Fix Your Under-Eyes in Just 7 Days?) + live countdown timer embedded in CTA button instead of banner

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Question title with dollar anchor (from AK) + personalized 3-step processing echoing quiz answers
Positive alert framing (from AK) + reservation timer repositioned above CTA button

variants_tested: AK: 8.52% CVR, $2.04 EPC (869 clicks) — trending better but p=0.74
AL: 6.90% CVR, $1.66 EPC (319 clicks) — cut day 1

results.significance: no — early cut, 1 day of data. AM probable loser (28% below AA). AN within noise but below baseline.

cycles.campaign: Wesley - Refi 5 (LeadPoint) - Facebook

challengers.description: Full-screen step flow (Rocket Mortgage UX + gov aesthetic). One question per screen, thin progress bar, immersive centered layout, dashboard-panel result page.
Official determination notice. Document/letter format with reference number, date, inline pill verification, formal determination section with bordered amount box.

previous_cycle_results.significance: no — early cut, 1 day of data
variants_tested: AM: 5.02% CVR, $1.21 EPC (219 clicks) — probable loser, 28% below AA
AN: 6.20% CVR, $1.49 EPC (258 clicks) — below baseline
